text: Working with APIs is a common task in modern software development. APIs communicate using standard HTTP requests, such as GET and POST, which allow you to retrieve or send data. The information is often structured in JSON format, a lightweight way to store data. Before accessing many APIs, you must set up authentication, usually with an API key. This key identifies you and controls your access. Additionally, most APIs enforce rate limits, which restrict the number of requests you can make within a certain time. Exceeding these limits can block your access temporarily. Finally, always include error handling in your code. APIs may return error codes, and your program should manage them gracefully to avoid crashes or unexpected behavior.
